title: WhatsApp → Safety Observation
subtitle: Demo 2 · site screenshots, integrated to Forma

inputs.label: WhatsApp Screenshots
inputs.description: Screenshots from a site WhatsApp group — containing chat messages, on-site photos, and informal hazard discussions captured by the team.
inputs.inputFormat: One or more screenshot images containing a mix of chat text and embedded site photos.

persona.quote: The team flags hazards in our site chat group — I screenshot the thread and turn it into a proper safety record before anything gets missed.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Reads the screenshots and separates the conversation text from the embedded site photos.

agents.name: Conversation Summary
agents.description: Reads the WhatsApp screenshots, extracts the chat thread, and produces a concise summary of the reported issue and its context.
agents.output: A structured conversation summary: reported issue, location, time, and involved parties.

agents.name: Image Analysis
agents.description: Applies computer vision to the extracted site photos to identify hazard objects, unsafe conditions, and relevant scene details.
agents.output: Detected hazard objects and conditions identified in the photos.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Retrieves the official hazard taxonomy and assigns the correct category to the identified hazard.

agents.name: SGBuildex
agents.description: Queries the SGBuildex API to retrieve the official hazard categories and safety observation taxonomy applicable to this project.
agents.output: The applicable hazard taxonomy: category list, severity levels, and required fields.

agents.name: Hazard Identification
agents.description: Maps the hazard objects detected by Image Analysis to the correct official category from the SGBuildex taxonomy.
agents.output: A classified hazard: official category, severity, and description.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Creates the safety observation form in Autodesk and attaches the photo evidence.

agents.name: Form
agents.description: Creates and populates the Autodesk Forms safety observation record, using the conversation summary and classified hazard data. The form can be reviewed in Claude Chat or auto-submitted.
agents.output: A completed safety observation form in Autodesk, ready for review or submission.

agents.name: Relationship
agents.description: Attaches the site photo to the form as evidence, with proper metadata linkage to keep the record traceable.
agents.output: Photo attached to the observation form with metadata relationship.

stations.label: GOVERN
stations.description: Triggers the data pipeline to refresh the management Power BI dashboard with the latest safety statistics.

agents.name: Dashboard
agents.description: Triggers the downstream data pipeline to refresh the Power BI safety dashboard, ensuring management sees up-to-date site observation statistics and trends.
agents.output: Power BI dashboard refreshed — latest safety observation counts and categories visible to management.

outputs.label: Safety Observation
outputs.description: A classified, evidence-linked safety observation created in Autodesk Forma.

outputs.label: Power BI Dashboard
outputs.description: Management dashboard updated with the latest site safety statistics and observation trends.

steps.caption: A site safety engineer screenshots a hazard discussion from the team's WhatsApp group.
Screenshots arrive — a mix of chat messages and on-site photos of a reported hazard.
The Conversation Summary Agent reads the thread; Image Analysis scans the photos for hazard objects.
SGBuildex retrieves the official hazard taxonomy; Hazard Identification assigns the correct category.
The Form Agent creates the Autodesk observation record; Relationship attaches the photo as evidence.
The Dashboard Agent triggers the data pipeline — management's Power BI view updates in real time.
A classified, evidence-linked safety observation is created in Autodesk Forma.
The Power BI dashboard refreshes — site safety statistics are current for management.
